results.title: Even Apple employees hate Siri and are skeptical of its future, new report says | Hacker News
results.content: using my voice.  I can write notes and messages.  I can switch to any app.  Voice control is a world leading accessibility feature that is a complete touch replacement controlled via voice.  Once I no longer need voice control I turn it off via Siri. The entire action of turning it on, using it, turning it off occurs with no touch at any point.  Also it's great for when I am cooking or doing diy and don't want to touch my phone.  In comparison Siri is better at a few narrowly defined actions but has less than 1% of the scope of voice control.  Reading people's complaints about Siri in this thread I feel what most people actually want is voice control.  But no one outside the visually impaired community knows about it. | | | |  |  | johnny22 on April 27, 2023  | parent | prev | next [...] to Australian. Don’t set Language to something that isn’t your accent! | | | |  |  | idk1 on April 27, 2023  | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Two pretty consistent frustrating issues I have are: Me: "Hey Siri, tell my Mike I'll send that document over in ten mins. How about pizza for lunch. I also spoke to Jenny and she would come for lunch too."  Siri: "Calling mike."  So cannot tell the difference between 'tell' and 'call', however Siri could use context and assume if I'm talking for quite a while after saying maybe tell or maybe call then it's most likely 'tell' because of the sentences afterwards. This is by far the most frustrating.  Second issue is:  Me: "Hey Siri - what's in my calendar today."  Siri: "Playing alternative radio station."  Such a tremendous amount of times music [...] |  | mrbombastic on April 27, 2023  | parent | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Yes, incredibly frustrating that to ask Siri to play a song from your library on your device you need a network connection. | | | |  |  | s3p on April 29, 2023  | parent | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Except they already do this? Since iOS 15, Siri has used on-device recognition. That's why you can go into Airplane mode and do things like ask Siri to change the brightness. | | | |  |  | epaga on April 28, 2023  | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Siri is without a doubt in my mind the most embarrassing thing Apple has had on the market in the past 20 years. With ChatGPT it is now feeling like Siri the broken tricycle is being compared to a Lamborghini.  Honestly I would have been concerned if Apple

results.title: If Siri isn't working on your iPhone or iPad - Apple Support
results.content:
## Set up Siri to recognize your voice

1. Go to Settings.
2. Tap Apple Intelligence & Siri, or tap Siri. Then tap Talk & Type to Siri.
3. Turn off Listen for "Siri" or "Hey Siri," then turn it back on.
4. When the Siri setup screen appears, tap Continue.
5. Say each command that you see on your screen.
6. Tap Done.

## If you can't hear Siri

1. Go to Settings.
2. Tap Apple Intelligence & Siri.
3. Tap Siri Responses.
4. Tap Prefer Spoken Responses to hear Siri speak responses, even when Silent mode is turned on.

   Siri Responses screen with 

You can also change Siri's speaking volume and speed.

## Update your software

Siri requires iOS 7 or later. If your device is using an older operating system, update the software on your iPhone or iPad to continue using Siri. [...] # If Siri isn't working on your iPhone or iPad

Check a few settings on your iPhone or iPad to make sure that Siri is set up to listen for your voice and respond to your requests.

## Turn on "Siri" or "Hey Siri"

1. Go to Settings.
2. Tap Apple Intelligence & Siri, or tap Siri. Then tap Talk & Type to Siri.
3. Make sure "Siri" or "Hey Siri" is turned on. If it's already on, turn it off and back on again.

   Talk & Type to Siri panel open, showing 

Restart your device. If Apple Intelligence & Siri or Siri & Search doesn't appear in Settings after your device restarts, check your VPN settings. Some VPN profiles might not allow use of Siri.

## Set up Siri to recognize your voice [...] ## Get more help with Siri

Find out how to use Siri on other devices.

Siri availability, features, and commands varies by language, and country and region. Learn which Siri features are supported in your country or region.

Published Date:

Thanks for your feedback.

results.title: Siri keeps devolving and it is frustrating as hell : r/HomeKit
results.content: This happens from time to time, sometimes with months in between. I think this is actually due to a failure happening in the Siri cloud backend.

results.title: Why Siri Still Sucks
results.content:
Sitemap

Open in app

Sign up

Sign in

.

Saying Siri sucks shouldn’t be seen as disparaging for a software that simply doesn't work a lot of the time. And it’s safe to assume that Apple knows this too. That’s why the thing has been revamped and “redesigned” more times than we can count.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image 4

Siri throughout the decades (2011 to date)

Perhaps the most frustrating thing about Siri is that since its launch, we’ve seen other attempts — and better too — from other companies. [...] We’ve seen Google Assistant (2016), and Cortana, from its launch (April 2014) to its untimely demise (2021–2023). Bixby launched in 2017, and even its predecessor, the dinosaur S Voice, launched in May 2012, more than six months after Siri was released. Alexa came into the market in November 2014.

ChatGPT, which launched in 2022, released an advanced AI voice feature in 2024 that could essentially describe the meaning of life in succinct detail. You could interrupt it to request a lullaby sang in Jamaican Patois.

I fixed this by changing my default language setting from English (UK) to English (US), which got it working. I then changed it back to English (UK) and it continued to work as expected.

Hope this helps someone.

I have reset it back to US English, and now it’s working like before. The question is how did this setting change after upgrade automatically?
